text:
REGIME — Risk-on growth and metals led; energy sold off hard.

INDICES — VIX 18.4, falling; VIXY -1.78%. QQQ +1.82%, IWM +1.38%, SPY +1.22%, DIA +0.70%. Large caps outpaced small caps on the day. Silver surged +5.51%, the standout cross-asset move, with gold adding +2.23% while oil (USO) collapsed -3.60%.

SECTORS — XLY +2.21%, XLK +1.60%, XLC +1.52%, XLRE +0.95%, XLV +0.58%, XLI +0.36%, XLF +0.23%, XLU +0.17%, XLP -0.10%, XLB -0.34%, XLE -2.03%. Top industries: WGMI +6.43%, SLV +5.51%; bottom industries: FCG -3.30%, USO -3.60%.

THE 7s — Mag 7 +3.01%, Cloud 7 +2.42%, New Staples 7 +2.42%, Fab 7 +2.19%, Old Metals 7 +1.38%, Semi 7 +1.35%, Medtech 7 +0.98%, Buildout 7 +0.96%, New Metals 7 +0.66%, Power 7 +0.64%, Freight 7 +0.62%, Bank 7 +0.35%, Health 7 +0.34%, Industrial 7 +0.01%, Defense 7 -0.01%, Housing 7 -0.07%, Old Staples 7 -0.19%, Toll Road 7 -0.35%, Insurance 7 -0.64%, Software 7 -0.66%, Energy 7 -2.51%. Strongest ticker in Mag 7: ORCL +4.74% (vol spike 2.5x). Weakest in Energy 7: USO -3.60%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29; CME holds at 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike. Fed tally: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress 38/100 (Normal); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile; TED spread benign at 0.09%, ON-RRP near zero at $0.3B.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top movers: SLV +5.51% (cross-asset), WFC -5.70% (Bank 7), ORCL +4.74% (Mag 7). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.73%, worst XLP -3.59%; best basket Semi 7 +22.21%, worst Software 7 -12.64%. WFC dropped -5.70% on mixed Q1 results despite an EPS beat, with 2.1x volume confirming institutional distribution.

WATCH — WFC: -5.70%, 2.1x volume; EPS beat failed to hold — watch whether financials gap fills or selling extends into earnings season. RSPM: -0.40% on 5.4x volume spike with no clear catalyst; unusual volume without price confirmation warrants scrutiny for follow-through. SLV: +5.51% but still -4.5% below recent high; momentum break above resistance or reversal here sets tone for metals complex.
